rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the component type scope when contracts relate to component ports
Use implementation scopes for annex blocks that reference subcomponents or implementation-specific connections
Preserve the original AADL architecture by only inserting annex blocks in valid scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annex blocks unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Ensure annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to the component hierarchy
Preserve existing component types and implementations when inserting annex blocks
Do not modify the AADL architecture beyond inserting annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use valid component ports in component type annex blocks
Use subcomponents and connections in implementation annex blocks when appropriate
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that reference subcomponents in component type annex blocks
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annex blocks unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not modify the AADL architecture beyond inserting annex blocks
irrelevant_topics: Type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
